headline: 15 core protocol contracts catalogued on Polygon (Chain ID 137); deployment addresses and audit PDFs sourced from official documentation
short_headline: 15 contracts on Polygon; addresses + audits verified

findings.text: Official Polymarket docs confirm all contracts deployed on Polygon mainnet (Chain ID 137) only; single source of truth for contract addresses maintained at https://docs.polymarket.com/resources/contracts
Deployed contracts documentation page located at https://docs.polymarket.com/resources/contracts with complete address table for core trading, collateral, wallet factory, and resolution contracts
Two independent audits identified and sourced: Quantstamp (March 2026) and Cantina (March 2026) for CTF Exchange V2; bug bounty program via Cantina
GitHub organization confirmed at https://github.com/polymarket with source repositories for ctf-exchange-v2, ctf-exchange, and additional tooling
Single chain deployment (Polygon); no multi-chain enumeration required
No factory enumeration required; core contracts explicitly listed in official documentation with no child contract enumeration indicated

rationale.verdict: Discovery catalogues 15 core Polymarket protocol contracts across Polygon mainnet from official documentation and GitHub sources. All contract addresses, roles, and deployment metadata sourced from https://docs.polymarket.com/resources/contracts (D2 source) and verified via Polygon block explorer name-tag searches. Two audits (Quantstamp and Cantina, both March 2026) referenced in official docs. GitHub organization established at https://github.com/polymarket. No multi-chain or factory child enumeration applies. Grade is 'unknown' per discovery slice specification.

evidence.shows: Official Polymarket documentation listing all 15 deployed contract addresses on Polygon mainnet (Chain ID 137) across four categories: Core Trading (CTF Exchange, Neg Risk CTF Exchange, Neg Risk Adapter, Conditional Tokens), Collateral (pUSD proxy/impl, CollateralOnramp, CollateralOfframp, PermissionedRamp, CtfCollateralAdapter, NegRiskCtfCollateralAdapter), Wallet Factory (Gnosis Safe Factory, Polymarket Proxy Factory), and Resolution (UMA Adapter, UMA Optimistic Oracle). Also lists Quantstamp and Cantina audit PDFs and Cantina bug bounty program link.
Official Polymarket GitHub organization with repositories including ctf-exchange-v2, ctf-exchange, clob-client-v2, py-clob-client-v2, polymarket-cli, and agents framework.
Quantstamp audit report for CTF Exchange V2 (March 2026) referenced and linkable from official docs
Cantina audit report for CTF Exchange V2 (March 2026) referenced and linkable from official docs

unknowns: D7: Actor class (EOA vs multisig) and detailed governance structure for contract admin/owner roles not yet determined from docs alone; requires on-chain reads

protocol_metadata.about: Polymarket is a decentralized prediction market platform enabling users to trade binary and multi-outcome markets on real-world events. Users deposit USDC collateral (via pUSD) to mint conditional tokens representing market outcomes, then trade these tokens through a hybrid exchange model with on-chain settlement via the CTF Exchange. The platform uses Polygon for low-cost, high-throughput transactions and UMA's Optimistic Oracle for market resolution.
